data.impact: Baseline change that everything downstream builds on.

nodes.description:
Team brought AI coding assistance into the daily workflow — code generation,
review, and test scaffolding.
nodes.title: Adopted AI-assisted development

data.hypothesis:
The freed capacity is enough to design and prove a safe automatic-update
mechanism (staged rollout + rollback) for the product.
data.impact: Validated — staged rollout with rollback proven on a test fleet.
Reduced manual release toil; faster, safer delivery to users.

nodes.description:
Automatic updates went to production with staged rollout and rollback — a
capability that would normally never have cleared the backlog.
nodes.title: Shipped automatic updates

data.impact: Created budget headroom without adding headcount.

nodes.description:
Cycle time from idea to working change dropped sharply once AI assistance was
embedded in the loop. This is the slack that funded everything in the later
phases.
nodes.title: Iteration speed roughly tripled

data.impact: Made the expansion-phase platform work possible.

nodes.description:
With the core roadmap ahead of schedule, engineers had time to invest in
foundational work that is usually deferred indefinitely.
nodes.title: Freed engineering capacity

data.impact: De-risked the automatic-update experiment before it started.

nodes.description:
Used AI to generate a broad regression suite quickly, raising confidence
enough to attempt riskier platform work later.
nodes.title: AI-generated regression harness

data.impact: New capability delivered at no incremental budget.

nodes.description:
Shipped a working simulator that was originally cut from scope for budget
reasons. The AI-driven iteration speed is what made it affordable.
nodes.title: Delivered the simulator

data.hypothesis:
With AI-accelerated development, a simulator we previously scoped out as too
expensive can be prototyped within the existing budget.
data.impact: Validated — prototype came together in days, not the projected weeks.

nodes.title: Spike — is a hardware simulator feasible now?

tracks.description: Foundational capabilities unlocked by freed capacity.
tracks.title: Platform

tree.description:
A delivery-narrative graph: how adopting AI-assisted development let one
project iterate fast enough to deliver a simulator that was originally out
of budget, and freed capacity to explore automatic updates. Nodes are
events, experiments, and milestones — not skills. Same engine, different
profile (see docs/overview.md). Compile with `--profile delivery`.
tree.title: AI-Accelerated Delivery
